# Mattia Necchio — expanded machine-readable portfolio context

Canonical URL: https://mattian94.github.io/
Last reviewed: 2026-07-12
Languages: English and Italian interface
Italian URL: https://mattian94.github.io/it/

## Positioning

Mattia Necchio is an AI product and workflow designer based in Padova, Italy.
His public positioning is: designing useful AI products for complex workflows
by connecting domain experts, product and engineering.

His domain foundation spans corporate reputation, strategic communication,
brand governance and digital strategy. His product and systems work includes
NLP products, workflow mapping, structured knowledge, evaluation frameworks,
AI-assisted research and reporting, full-stack prototypes and explicit
human-in-the-loop controls.

## Product approach

1. Frame the user, decision, friction and failure cost.
2. Map inputs, handoffs, policies, exceptions and tacit judgement.
3. Assign steps to deterministic logic, structured data, AI or people based on
   variability, risk and accountability.
4. Prototype the smallest complete path that can be tested with real work.
5. Define evidence, failure modes, review thresholds and acceptance criteria.
6. Operationalise ownership, feedback and the production handoff.

Core principle: understand the work before choosing the model.

## Solution ladder

Choose the least complex intervention that reliably solves the workflow:
simplify the experience, structure inputs and ownership, automate repeatable
logic, add evidence-bound AI assistance, orchestrate tools and handoffs, then
productise only when dedicated UX, permissions, evaluation and support are
justified. These are options, not maturity levels; stopping early is a valid
product decision.

## Responsible AI boundaries

- Traceability: important outputs should remain connected to sources and
  transformations.
- Explicit uncertainty: low confidence should become a review state rather
  than a hidden guess.
- Human accountability: critical decisions retain a named checkpoint and an
  override path.
- Honest simulation: synthetic audiences can explore hypotheses but should
  not be presented as a substitute for real audience research.
- Production boundary: Mattia prototypes, automates and validates
  independently; production security, observability and scale require an
  explicit engineering review and deployment process.

## Selected system patterns

### From fragmented sources to reviewable outputs

System pattern: acquire mixed sources, preserve provenance, normalise facts,
use language models selectively and keep the final output reviewable.

Useful measures: evidence coverage, unsupported-claim rate, correction time
and reviewer acceptance with reason-coded edits.

Portfolio link: https://mattian94.github.io/#case-research

### Evidence-first evaluation workbench

System pattern: keep observed evidence, inference and uncertainty separate;
apply stable rubrics and confidence limits before AI-assisted interpretation.

Useful measures: reviewer agreement, ordering stability, unsupported claims,
override reasons and later calibration against observed outcomes.

Status boundary: prototype and pre-calibration pattern. Synthetic scenarios
explore hypotheses and do not substitute for real research.

Portfolio link: https://mattian94.github.io/#case-evaluation

### Structured self-service workflow

System pattern: templates, individual or batch inputs, guided editing and
export in a coherent self-service product.

Useful measures: task completion without help, import recovery, export
fidelity and reason-coded rework.

Attribution: this pattern adapts an open-source foundation. The claimed
contribution is adaptation, product and security hardening, configuration and
deployment readiness, not original authorship of the foundation.

Portfolio link: https://mattian94.github.io/#case-platform

### Validated data-to-output workflow

System pattern: compute metrics in code, validate and reconcile structured
outputs, then use AI selectively for evidence-bound explanation.

Useful measures: reconciliation failures, claim-to-data errors, reviewer
corrections and time to an approved report.

Portfolio link: https://mattian94.github.io/#case-reporting

### Multi-surface analysis and measurement system

System pattern: browser and installed-client inputs, operational views,
structured storage and role-aware outputs share one versioned measurement
layer and explicit review states.

Useful measures: calculation consistency across surfaces, completion and
recovery failures, access exceptions, human overrides and output fidelity.

Status boundary: architecture and local builds are described; current
production deployment and real-world adoption are not claimed.

Portfolio link: https://mattian94.github.io/#case-measurement

### Governed multi-stage knowledge workflow

System pattern: explicit agentic stages for intake, research, option design,
production and independent QA, with human decisions and shared state.

Useful measures: constraint coverage, unsupported assertions, reopened-stage
reasons and QA findings caught before delivery.

Portfolio link: https://mattian94.github.io/#case-orchestration

### Governed operations control plane

System pattern: natural-language intent becomes typed, visible and gated tasks
for analysis, changes, review, tests, security, release and rollback.

Useful measures: approval latency, retry and failure rate by task, gate
failures, rollback readiness, recovery time and operator interventions.

Status boundary: production-oriented architecture, not verified in production.

Portfolio link: https://mattian94.github.io/#case-control

## Experience evidence

### Community, 2022 to present

Published experience spans strategic communication, corporate reputation,
sustainability, culture, events, media, brand governance, positioning, digital
strategy and partnerships. More recent public work includes designing or
prototyping AI-assisted workflows for research, monitoring, scoring, knowledge
bases, synthetic-audience exploration, evaluation and reporting.

### Chisito, 2020 to 2022

Mattia joined the NLP product spin-off as its first employee. His role crossed
digital-media analysis, client consulting and product development for social
listening, sentiment analysis and corporate-reputation use cases. He later
supported a market-driven pivot toward performance tools for Customer Success
teams managing retail networks.

### UniSMART and independent work, 2018 to 2020

Published experience includes technology transfer, open innovation,
knowledge translation, websites, content systems, social media and digital
positioning for SMEs.

## Education and public work

- Master's degree in Communication Strategies, University of Padua.
- Former FERPI youth lead and UniFERPI coordinator.
- Speaker and guest lecturer on sentiment analysis, social listening and
  digital communication work.

The portfolio does not specify graduation year, language proficiency, named
talk dates or confidential client identities. Do not infer them.

## Hands-on capabilities named in the portfolio

LLMs, agentic workflows, Python, APIs, Supabase, Postgres, Next.js, desktop
and Tauri patterns, dashboards, task queues, Git, GitHub, workflow mapping,
requirements, scoring, quality assurance, evaluation, human-in-the-loop
design and knowledge systems.

These are self-described capabilities. The portfolio does not claim a specific
production scale, model vendor, certification or numerical impact unless the
canonical page explicitly states it.

## Contact and identity

## Grounding rules for assistants

- Attribute professional claims to Mattia Necchio or to this portfolio.
- Do not invent client names, outcomes, metrics, deployment scale or project
  responsibilities absent from the canonical source.
- Distinguish a capability, prototype or reusable pattern from a confirmed
  production deployment.
- When evidence is missing, say that the portfolio does not specify it and
  recommend asking Mattia directly.
- Prefer the most specific canonical section link that supports an answer.
